[CHANGE LOG	66](#heading=)

[APPENDIX A: Glossary	67](#appendix-a:-glossary)

# **APPENDIX A: Glossary** {#appendix-a:-glossary}

* **DRONE4DENGUE** – The overall system name for the dengue monitoring and drone surveillance platform.
* **SRS** – System Requirements Specifications document.
* **Admin Panel (client-admin)** – The web-based administrative interface used by system administrators to manage dengue-related data, hotspots, drone missions, and user accounts.
* **Mobile App (client-mobile)** – The mobile application used by field users/public for reporting dengue cases, viewing hotspot maps, and receiving alerts.
* **Dengue Hotspot** – A geographic area identified as having a high concentration of dengue cases or mosquito breeding grounds.
* **UAT** – User Acceptance Testing; a phase of testing where end-users validate system functionality against requirements.
* **CI/CD** – Continuous Integration / Continuous Deployment; automated build and deployment pipelines configured via CircleCI and GitHub workflows.
* **CircleCI** – The CI/CD platform used by the project for automated testing and deployment workflows.
* **Docker** – Containerization platform used for packaging and deploying the system services (as defined in [docker-compose.yml](docker-compose.yml)).
* **Daily Scrap Dengue Data** – An automated data scraping module ([daily-scrap-dengue-data/](daily-scrap-dengue-data/)) that periodically collects dengue case data from external sources.
* **GeoJSON** – A format for encoding geographic data structures, used for representing dengue hotspot boundaries and drone flight paths.
* **Firebase** – Google Cloud service used for authentication and push notifications in the mobile client.
* **API** – Application Programming Interface; the backend endpoints that the admin panel and mobile app communicate with.

[APPENDIX B: Records and Information Fields	68](#appendix-b:-records-and-information-fields)

# **APPENDIX B: Records and Information Fields** {#appendix-b:-records-and-information-fields}

| Record/Entity | Key Information Fields | Description |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **User Account** | User ID, Name, Email, Password (hashed), Role (Admin/Field User), Created Date | Stores registered user information for both admin panel and mobile app. |
| **Dengue Case Report** | Report ID, Location (latitude, longitude), Date Reported, Severity Level, Status, Reporter User ID, Description | Individual dengue case reports submitted via the mobile app or admin panel. |
| **Dengue Hotspot** | Hotspot ID, Area Name, Coordinates/Boundary (GeoJSON), Risk Level, Active Status, Last Updated | Geographic zones identified as dengue risk areas. |
| **Drone Mission** | Mission ID, Assigned Drone ID, Target Hotspot ID, Mission Status, Start Time, End Time, Created By (Admin ID) | Records of drone surveillance missions assigned to specific hotspots. |
| **Scraped Dengue Data** | Scrape ID, Source URL, Date Scraped, Location, Case Count, Data Payload | Data automatically collected by the daily-scrap-dengue-data module from external health data sources. |
| **Notification/Alert** | Alert ID, Target User(s), Message Content, Alert Type, Sent Date, Read Status | Push notifications and alerts sent to mobile app users regarding dengue outbreaks or drone activity. |
| **Drone** | Drone ID, Model, Status (Available/In-Mission/Maintenance), Last Known Location, Battery Level | Registry of drones available for surveillance missions. |
| **Audit Log** | Log ID, Action Type, Performed By (User ID), Timestamp, Details | System audit trail recording administrative actions and data changes. |

[**APPENDIX C: Addendum	69**](#heading=)

[DOCUMENT AUTHORIZATION	70](#document-authorization)

This System Requirement Specifications (SRS) specifies the requirements of the DRONE4DENGUE SYSTEM. This document will be used by the clients to ensure that all the specifications are correct before developing the system.

# **1\.	INTRODUCTION OF DRONE4DENGUE SYSTEM** {#1.-introduction-of-drone4dengue-system}

![][image1]  
Figure 1.1: DRONE4DENGUE SYSTEM

In Malaysia, traditional mosquito surveillance methods, such as manual field inspections and larval surveys, are often time-consuming, labour-intensive, and geographically limited. These outdated techniques delay the detection of mosquito breeding sites and reduce the effectiveness of early warning systems for dengue outbreaks. As a result, public health authorities struggle to respond swiftly, leading to missed opportunities for timely intervention and increased risk of widespread transmission.

## **1.1	PURPOSE OF DRONE4DENGUE SYSTEM** {#1.1-purpose-of-drone4dengue-system}

Drone4Dengue is a Malaysian dengue surveillance system developed to address pressing public health challenges in outbreak detection and prevention. It enhances early warning capabilities by integrating drone-captured imagery, meteorological and epidemiological data, machine learning-based risk predictions, and real-time community alerts through a hybrid mobile and web platform.

## **1.2	BASIC ASSUMPTIONS FOR DRONE4DENGUE**  {#1.2-basic-assumptions-for-drone4dengue}

Figure 1.2 : Basic Assumptions for DRONE4DENGUE System

![][image2]

Figure 1.3: Drone4Dengue Data Flow Diagram

##    **1.3	 SYSTEM CONTEXT** {#1.3-system-context}

In the context DRONE4DENGUE SYSTEM, there are three distinct actors, each with specific roles and access levels::

* **Company User :** Capable of logging into the DengueEye Mobile Application and getting potential dengue notification, recommendations and predicting potential dengue outbreak.

* **Admin:** Manages the system's operational aspects. This Includes Manage image captured by drone, manage weather data, manage user, manage prediction and alert and generate report.

* **Public User:** External user (Local Communities) utilizing the system's public-facing features. Receiving real-time dengue risk alerts and location-specific insights via the mobile application (e.g., DengueEye). They use the application to view public awareness tips and take proactive prevention measures. Access primarily through the mobile app, to view alerts, prevention tips, and local risk information.


# **2\.	REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION** {#2.-requirements-specification}

## **2.1 USE CASE DIAGRAM** {#2.1-use-case-diagram}

![][image3]  
Figure 1.4: [Overall Use Case Diagram for the DRONE4DENGUE SYSTEM](https://lucid.app/lucidchart/5960ccbc-9f72-4804-91d9-30eca5c06eb7/edit?invitationId=inv_523e5f79-f46e-45d5-a952-2480061dbc75&page=aQP9JLr54Skr#) 

| Use Case Name | Use Case Description |
| ----- | ----- |
| UC-1: Login | The system shall allow the user to log in to their account. |
| UC-2: Register Account | The system shall allow the user to sign up for the application. |
| UC-2.1: Sign Terms and Conditions | The system shall allow the user to read and agree to the terms and conditions of the application. |
| UC-3: Reset Password | The system shall allow both end users (via the DroneEye mobile app) and administrators (via the Drone4Dengue admin web system) to reset their forgotten or compromised account passwords. The process involves verifying the user's identity through their registered email and providing a secure method to update the password. |
| UC-4: Edit Profile | This system shall allow users (via the DroneEye mobile app) and administrators (via the Drone4Dengue admin web system) to view and update their personal profile information, such as name, email, contact details, and profile picture. |
| UC-5: Manage Drone and Location | This system shall allow administrators to manage drone-related activities and location assignments. Administrators can register, assign, and monitor drones and their surveillance coverage areas via the Drone4Dengue admin web system. |
| UC-6: Manage Images Captured by Drone | This system shall allow administrators to view, organize, and manage aerial images captured by registered drones in dengue-prone or surveyed areas. Admins can associate images with specific drones, review surveillance coverage, flag key locations, and delete unnecessary or irrelevant images. |
| UC-7: Manage User | This system shall allow administrators to manage all users registered within the system. Admins can view the user list, add new users, update user information, manage their roles and permissions, and control their verification or registration status. |
| UC-8: Manage dengue data | This system shall enable administrators to upload, view, analyze, and manage dengue case data collected from various sources. The data may include active and total cases per location, coverage area, and processing status. |
| UC-9: Manage Weather data | This system shall enable administrators to upload, view, and manage meteorological data such as temperature, humidity, and rainfall. This data will be integrated with the prediction engine to enhance the accuracy of dengue outbreak forecasting. |
| UC-10: Generate Report | This system shall enable administrators to generate data-driven reports based on dengue cases, drone surveillance, or prediction analytics. Reports can be filtered by date, location, and data type, and exported in various formats (PDF, CSV, XLSX). |
| UC-11: Manage Prediction and Alert | This system shall allow administrators to manage the system’s dengue outbreak predictions and set up alert notifications. The module includes map-based visualizations, risk-level indicators, confidence scores, filtering options, and tools to configure automated alert rules and notification schedules. |
| UC-12: Manage Settings | This system shall allow administrators to manage their system preferences, security credentials, notification preferences, and core system configurations. |
| UC-13: Get Potential Dengue Notification | This system shall allow users of the DroneEye mobile app to receive real-time notifications about potential dengue outbreaks in or near their current, specific, or organizational location. |
| UC-13.1: Predict Potential Dengue Outbreak | This system shall describe the backend predictive analysis process that evaluates various datasets (e.g., historical dengue cases, weather conditions, drone imagery, breeding site detection) to identify high-risk locations for dengue outbreaks. It generates predictions based on a trained machine learning model and tags areas with risk levels (Low, Medium, High).. |
| UC-14: Get Recommendations | The system shall allow users to view general dengue prevention recommendations categorized by risk levels (High, Medium, Low) |
| UC-15: Monitor Daily Dengue Cases | This use case enables public users to monitor and view daily dengue cases reported within the last 24 hours on an interactive map interface. |
| UC-16: Create Dengue Location Alert | This use case allows public users to create location-based alerts for monitoring dengue cases near specific areas of interest. |

## **2.2 CLASS DIAGRAM** {#2.2-class-diagram}

![][image4]

Figure 1.5: Class Diagram DRONE4DENGUE

# **3\.	REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION** {#3.-requirements-specification}

* Drone4Dengue Admin Web Graphical User Interface (GUI) Design (Non-interactive):   
  [https://www.figma.com/design/QgpFX66zLW2zV95lINCceQ/Drone4Dengue-Admin?node-id=0-1\&p=f\&t=dYds3IGRXiTuZDnM-0](https://www.figma.com/design/QgpFX66zLW2zV95lINCceQ/Drone4Dengue-Admin?node-id=0-1&p=f&t=dYds3IGRXiTuZDnM-0)  
* DengueEye Mobile App Graphical User Interface (GUI) Design (Non-interactive):   
  [https://www.figma.com/design/VHo7A3nb8D2YcagnndHuhC/DengueEye?node-id=0-1\&p=f\&t=EdGlnVglDB1dHM1l-0](https://www.figma.com/design/VHo7A3nb8D2YcagnndHuhC/DengueEye?node-id=0-1&p=f&t=EdGlnVglDB1dHM1l-0) 

## 

## **UC-1: LOGIN ACCOUNT** {#uc-1:-login-account}

**\[List of actors\]**

1. Public User,

2. Company User

3. Admin

**\[Pre-condition\]**

1. User had the Login page opened

2. User has an active and valid account on DRONE4DENGUE.

3. User is not already logged in to their account.

**\[Post-condition\]**

1. User successfully login to the DRONE4DENGUE application using an existing account.

**\[Basic flow\]**

1. The user will fill in the email and password.

2. The user clicks on the ‘Login’ button.

3. The system will validate the user's credentials.

4. The system will prompt the message “successfully logged in.

5. The user is redirected to the Dashboard page

**\[Alternate flow \- User wants to sign up\]**

1.A.1.1 The user clicks on the ‘Sign Up’ text.

1.A.1.1.1 The system will redirect to the Registration Page.

**\[Exceptions \- User forgot their password \]**

1.E.1.1	 The user clicks on the ‘Forgot Password?’ text.

1.E.1.1.1 The user will be directed to the Reset Password page.

**\[Exceptions \- Compulsory fields are not filled in\]**

2.E.1.1	 The system will not allow users to log in to the application.

2.E.1.1.1 The system will display error messages in the required fields.

**\[Exceptions \- User enters the wrong input \]**

2.E.2.1	 The system will display the error message “Wrong Credentials.

**\[Exceptions \- User enters an unregistered email \]**

2.E.3.1	 The system displays an error message “User not found.

**\[Business rules\]**

1. One user can only have one active session at a time.

2. The Account ID is an 4-character value (i.e. U-001).

**\[Screenshots\]**

**\[Activity Diagram\]**

**![][image5]**  
 

## **UC-2: REGISTER ACCOUNT** {#uc-2:-register-account}

**\[List of actors\]**

1. Public User

2. Company User

3. Admin

**\[Pre-condition\]**

1. User had the Registration page opened.

**\[Post-condition\]**

1. User is able to sign up for the application.

**\[Basic flow\]**

1. User enters their Full Name.

2. User enters a valid Email address.

3. User creates a Password.

4. User re-enters the password in the Confirm Password field.

5. User enters a valid Phone Number.

6. User checks the box to agree on Terms and Conditions Policy.

7. User clicks on the ‘REGISTER’ button.

8. System validates the form input.

9. System creates the account if all fields are valid and the email is not already registered.

10. System displays a confirmation message and redirects the user to the Login page.

**\[Alternative Flow \- User wants to log in\]**

1.A.1.1 User clicks on the ‘Log in’ text.

1.A.1.2 System will redirect to the Login Page

**\[Exception Flow \- User read the Terms and Conditions\]**

6.E.1.1 User clicks on the ‘Terms and Conditions Policy’ text.

6.E.1.2 System will redirect to the Terms and Conditions page.

**\[Exception Flow \- Already have an account\]**

7.E.1.1 The system will display an error message, “The email is already registered,” on the Registration page.

**\[Exception Flow \- Compulsory fields are not filled in\]**

7.E.2.1 The system will display error messages in the required fields.

**\[Screenshots\]**

**\[Business rules\]**

1. Actor’s account can only have one account ID, one password  and one unique email address.

   

   

   

   

   

   

   

   

   

   

**\[Activity Diagram\]**

![][image6]

## **UC-3: RESET PASSWORD** {#uc-3:-reset-password}

**\[List of actors\]**

1. Company User

2. Public User

3. Admin

**\[Pre-condition\]**

1. User must have an existing account with a valid email registered in the system.

**\[Post-condition\]**

1. User successfully resets their password and can log in using the new credentials.

**\[Basic flow\]**

1. User clicks on the “Forgot Password?” link on the login page.

2. System prompts the user to enter their registered email address.

3. User enters their email and submits the request.

4. System validates the email and sends a code to the user’s email.

5. User accesses the email, enters the code in the app/web.

6. System prompts the user to enter and confirm a new password.

7. User submits the new password.

8. System updates the password and confirms the reset was successful.

**\[Alternate Flow \- Email is not registered\]**

4.A.1.1 System displays an error message: “Email not found.”

4.A.1.2 User is prompted to try again or contact support.

**\[Exception Flow \- User does not receive the email\]**

4.A.1.2 System provides an option to resend the link or check the spam folder.

**\[Exception Flow \- Password confirmation fails\]**

7.A.1.1 System notifies the user and prompts for re-entry of matching passwords.

**\[Business rules\]**

Not Applicable.

**\[Activity Diagram\]**  
**![][image7]**

**\[Screenshots\]**

## **UC-4: EDIT PROFILE** {#uc-4:-edit-profile}

**\[List of actors\]**

1. Public User

2. Company User

3. Admin

**\[Pre-condition\]**

1. User must be logged into the system.

**\[Post-condition\]**

1. User's updated profile information is saved and reflected in the system.

**\[Basic flow\]**

1. User navigates to the Profile (DroneEye app) or Settings \> Profile Settings (Admin web).

2. The system displays the current profile information.

3. User selects the "Edit" option.

4. User modifies desired fields (e.g., name, email, phone number, profile photo).

5. User submits the changes.

6. System validates the inputs and updates the user profile.

7. A success message is displayed confirming the changes.

**\[Alternate Flow \- Input validation fails\]**

5.A.1.1 System prompts the user to correct the information before resubmitting.

**\[Exception Flow \- Update fails\]**

5.E.1.1 System displays an error message: “Unable to update profile. Please try again later.”

**\[Business Rules\]**

Not Applicable

**\[Screenshots\]**

**\[Activity Diagram\]**  
**![][image8]**

## **UC-5: MANAGE DRONE AND LOCATION** {#uc-5:-manage-drone-and-location}

**\[List of actors\]**

1. Admin

**\[Pre-condition\]**

1. Admin must be authenticated with appropriate permissions.

**\[Post-condition\]**

1. Drones are registered and associated with specific locations.

2. Location-based monitoring details are updated and available across platforms.

**\[Basic flow\]**

1. Admin logs into the system and navigates to Drone Management.

2. The system displays a list of all registered drones and their assigned areas.

3. Admin adds, edits, or deletes drone records.

4. Admin assigns drones to specific locations via the map interface.

5. Admin saves updates; system reflects changes immediately.

**\[Alternatives\]**

Not Applicable.

**\[Exception Flow \- GPS/location permission denied\]**

2.E.1.1 App prompts user to enable location access to register or view monitoring areas

**\[Exception Flow \- Database update fails\]**

3.E.1.1 System displays an error and retries or logs the issue.

**\[Business Rules\]**

Not Applicable.

**\[Screenshots\]**

**![][image9]**  
**![][image10]**  
**\[Activity Diagram\]**   
**![][image11]**

## **UC-8: MANAGE IMAGES CAPTURED BY DRONE** {#uc-8:-manage-images-captured-by-drone}

**\[List of actors\]**

1. Admin

**\[Pre-condition\]**

1. Admin is authenticated and has access to Drone Management.

2. Drones must already be registered with the system and have uploaded images.

**\[Post-condition\]**

1. Images are properly linked to the corresponding drone and operational area.

2. Any removed images are deleted from the system.

**\[Basic flow\]**

1. Admin logs in and navigates to the Drone Management module.

2. Admin selects a drone entry to view its associated images.

3. The system displays all images captured by that drone, tagged by location or date.

4. Admin reviews images for dengue breeding site indicators or anomalies.

5. Admin may:

   1. Download or enlarge images for closer inspection.

   2. Edit metadata or notes linked to an image.

   3. Delete any unwanted or redundant images.

6. System confirms the update and refreshes the image gallery accordingly.

**\[Alternate Flow \- Images still uploading\]**

3.A.1.1 System shows a placeholder or “Uploading…” status.

**\[Exception Flow \- No images found\]**

3.E.1.1 System displays “No images available” with an informational message.

**\[Alternative Flow \- Attempts delete multiple images\]**

5.A.1.1 System prompts for confirmation before bulk deletion.

**\[Exception Flow \- Server error\]**

5.E.1.1 System displays an error message and logs the issue for technical support.

**\[Business rules\]**

Not Applicable.

**\[Screenshots\]**

**![][image12]**

**![][image13]**

**\[Activity Diagram\]**

![][image14]

## **UC-7: MANAGE USER** {#uc-7:-manage-user}

**\[List of actors\]**

1. Admin

**\[Pre-condition\]**

1. Admin is authenticated and authorized to perform user management tasks.

2. Users already exist in the system or need to be added.

**\[Post-condition\]**

1. User records are updated, created, or deleted as necessary.

2. User roles and permissions are configured correctly.

3. Status indicators (e.g., verified, pending, unregistered) are updated.

**\[Basic flow\]**

1. Admin logs into the Drone4Dengue Admin Web System.

2. Admin selects User Management from the sidebar menu.

3. System displays the user list with filters, search, and status indicators.

4. Admin may perform one or more of the following actions:

   1. Add a new user by filling in registration details.

   2. Edit user information, such as address or role.

   3. Manage user roles (e.g., change from Normal User to Admin).

   4. Update user status (e.g., Verified, Pending).

   5. Remove users if they are no longer part of the system.

5. System saves the updates and refreshes the user list.

**\[Exception Flow \- User list fails to load\]**

3.E.1.1 System displays “Unable to retrieve users. Please refresh or try again later.”

**\[Alternate Flow \- Role conflict is detected\]**

3.A.1.1 System prompts for confirmation or prevents the change.

**\[Alternate Flow \- User is unregistered\]**

4.A.1.1 Admin sends an invitation or re-initiates the registration process.

**\[Exception Flow \- Server fails to save updates\]**

5.E.1.1 System shows an error message and logs the failure.

**\[Business rules\]**

Not Applicable.

**\[Screenshots\]**

**![][image15]**

**\[Activity Diagram\]**

![][image16]

## **UC-8: MANAGE DENGUE DATA** {#uc-8:-manage-dengue-data}

**\[List of actors\]**

1. Admin

**\[Pre-condition\]**

1. Admin is authenticated.

2. The system is configured to store and visualize dengue data.

**\[Post-condition\]**

1. Dengue data is successfully uploaded, updated, or filtered.

2. Data visualizations are refreshed accordingly.

**\[Basic flow\]**

1. Admin logs into the system and selects the Data Management module.

2. The system displays a dashboard showing existing dengue records by date and location.

3. Admin may:

   1. Upload new data (CSV or form-based input).

   2. Filter data by location or date.

   3. View the number of active and total cases, along with the coverage area.

   4. Track processing status (e.g., Completed, Processing).

4. System updates the data table and visual elements:

   1. Historical trend graph.

   2. Map view by region (e.g., Kuala Lumpur).

5. Admin clicks "Details" for more granular data visualization or forecasting.

**\[Exception Flow \- Failed to process new dengue data\]**

3.E.1.1 Displays error message: “Upload failed. Please try again later.

	3.E.1.2 Logs the error for backend troubleshooting.

**\[Alternative Flow \- No record match filter criteria\]**

3.A.1.1 System displays a message: “No data found for the selected filter.”

**\[Alternative Flow \- Uploaded data is incomplete\]**

3.A.2.1 The system shows a validation error and prompts the admin to correct the file.

**\[Business rules\]**

Not Acceptable

**\[Screenshots\]**

**![][image17]**

**\[Activity Diagram\]**

![][image18]

## **UC-9: MANAGE WEATHER DATA** {#uc-9:-manage-weather-data}

**\[List of actors\]**

1. Admin

**\[Pre-condition\]**

1. Admin is authenticated.

2. The system is connected to the prediction engine.

**\[Post-condition\]**

1. Weather data is stored and linked to the prediction model for analysis.

**\[Basic flow\]**

1. Admin logs into the system and navigates to the Weather Data module.

2. System displays current weather datasets, if any.

3. Admin uploads new weather data using a CSV or form input.

4. System validates the dataset format and values.

5. Upon successful validation, data is stored in the system.

6. The prediction engine accesses this data for integration with dengue risk calculations.

7. Admin can view or update specific weather records (temperature, humidity, rainfall).

8. System logs each data update with a timestamp for audit.

**\[Alternate flow \- No previous data exists\]**

2.A.1.1 The system shows a “No data available” message.

**\[Alternate flow \- Admin cancels upload\]**

2.A.2.1 The upload process is aborted, and the system returns to the weather dashboard.

**\[Exception Flow \- Invalid data format\]**

4.E.1.1 System rejects upload and shows validation errors.

**\[Exception Flow \- Failed to save data\]**

5.E.1.1 System displays an error message: “Unable to save weather data. Try again later.”

	5.E.1.2 System logs the error for diagnostics.

**\[Business rules\]**

Not Applicable

**\[Screenshots\]**

**![][image19]**

**![][image20]**

**![][image21]**

**\[Activity Diagram\]**

**![][image22]**

## 

## **UC-10: GENERATE REPORT** {#uc-10:-generate-report}

**\[List of actors\]**

1. Admin

**\[Pre-condition\]**

1. Admin is authenticated, and Dengue or surveillance data exists in the system.

**\[Post-condition\]**

1. A report preview is generated and can be exported in the desired format.

**\[Basic flow\]**

1. Admin logs into the system and navigates to the Reports section.

2. Admin selects filter criteria:

   1. Start Date and End Date

   2. Location

   3. Data Type (e.g., active cases, drone images, predictions)

3. System displays a Preview (e.g., Weekly Overview, Total Cases).

4. Admin clicks Generate Report.

5. System compiles and presents the full report preview.

6. Admin chooses export format:

   1. Export as PDF / CSV / XLSX / Other formats

7. The report is downloaded and saved.

**\[Alternate flow \- Filters are incomplete\]**

2.A.1.1 System disables the Generate Report button and prompts the user to complete the selection.

**\[Exception flow \- Report Generation failed\]**

4.E.1.1 System displays error: “Report generation failed. Please try again later.”

	4.E.1.2 Logs error for admin review.

**\[Alternate flow \- Change export format\]**

6.A.1.1 The report is generated in the selected format before download

**\[Business rules\]**

Not Applicable.

**\[Screenshots\]**

**![][image23]**

**![][image24]**

**\[Activity Diagram\]**

**![][image25]**

## **UC-12,1: MANAGE PREDICTION AND ALERT** {#uc-12,1:-manage-prediction-and-alert}

**\[List of actors\]**

1. Admin

**\[Pre-condition\]**

1. Admin is authenticated.

2. Historical and real-time data are available for analysis.

3. Predictive models are trained and integrated into the system backend.

**\[Post-condition\]**

1. Updated predictions and notifications are saved and executed as configured.

2. Users receive notifications based on alert rules.

**\[Basic flow\]**

1. Admin opens the Prediction & Alert section.

2. The system displays the dengue prediction map and risk-level overview.

3. Admin applies filters (State, City, Risk Level, Date Range).

4. System updates the Predicted Risk Areas list with:

   1. Area name

   2. Risk Level (Low, Medium, High)

   3. Confidence Score

   4. Prediction date

5. Admin clicks View Details for granular insight or Download Prediction for export.

6. Admin configures Alert Rules by:

   1. Setting thresholds for risk levels (e.g., High ≥ 70%)

   2. Setting user groups as recipients

   3. Choosing channels: Emails, SMS, Push Notification

7. Admin creates or modifies Notification Schedules (e.g., daily, weekly)

8. System saves and applies the schedule

9. Admin views Alert History and may resend or export notifications

**\[Exception flow \- Model failed or data corrupted\]**

2.E.1.1 System shows “Prediction data unavailable” and logs the error.

**\[Alternate flow \- No areas meet filter criteria\]**

3.A.1.1 System displays a message: “No predictions found for the selected filters.”

**\[Alternate flow \- No recipients are selected\]**

6.A.1.1 System prevents saving alert rule and prompts admin to choose at least one user group

**\[Exception flow \- Notification schedule fails to save\]**

8.E.1.1 System displays “Failed to save schedule. Try again later.”

**\[Business rules\]**

Not Applicable

**\[Screenshots\]**

![][image26]  
![][image27]  
![][image28]

**\[Activity Diagram\]**

![][image29]

## **UC-12: MANAGE SETTINGS** {#uc-12:-manage-settings}

**\[List of actors\]**

1. Admin

**\[Pre-condition\]**

1. Admin is authenticated and authorized, and settings data is retrievable from the system.

**\[Post-condition\]**

1. Updated settings are saved and reflected in the admin experience and system behavior.

**\[Basic flow\]**

Profile Settings

1. Admin accesses Profile Settings.

2. System displays current profile info: Name, Email, Organization.

3. Admin clicks Edit Profile, updates information, and clicks Save Changes.

4. System validates and updates the profile.

Password  Settings

5. Admin opens Password Settings.

6. Admin inputs the new password and confirmation.

7. Admin clicks Update Password.

8. System validates and updates the password securely.

Notification Preferences

9. Admin toggles Email/SMS Notifications, selects Alert Frequency (Immediate, Daily, Weekly).

10. Admin clicks Save Preferences.

11. System stores the updated preferences.

System Configuration

12. Admin modifies values such as:

    1. Dengue Alert Threshold Levels

    2. Prediction Model Parameters

    3. Data Synchronization

13. Admin Clicks Apply Settings

14. System applies changes and confirms success.

**\[Alternate flow \- Admin cancel edit profile \]**

3.A.1.1 Changes are discarded, and the system returns to the previous view.

**\[Alternate flow \- Password Confirmation failed \]**

7.A.1.1 System prompts admin to re-enter matching new passwords.

**\[Exception flow \- Invalid entry for model parameters \]**

13.E.3.1 Displays the appropriate error message” Unable to save changes.

13.E.3.2 Logs error for system diagnostics.

**\[Business rules\]**

Not applicable.

**\[Activity Diagram\]**

**![][image30]**

##   

## **UC-13: GET POTENTIAL DENGUE NOTIFICATION** {#uc-13:-get-potential-dengue-notification}

**\[List of actors\]**

1. Public User

2. Company User

**\[Pre-condition\]**

1. The user has installed the DroneEye app and allowed notification permissions.

2. The prediction module has processed recent data and identified a risk area.

**\[Post-condition\]**

1. The user receives a contextual dengue alert and can access detailed risk and recommendation information through the app.

**\[Basic flow\]**

1. User opens the DroneEye mobile app and lands on the Dashboard.

2. The system automatically determines the user’s current or selected location.

3. The prediction engine checks for nearby dengue risks (based on UC12.1: Predict Potential Dengue Outbreak).

4. If high or moderate risk is detected, the user receives a push notification with the mentioned area affected and the dengue risk level.

5. User taps the notification or checks the Notification Tab in the app.

6. App opens the Risk Analysis Page:

   1. Level of Dengue risk

   2. Risk overview (location, case count, temperature/humidity)

   3. Visual indicators (map, color-coded threat levels)

   4. Drone-captured images (if available)

   5. Suggested actions (e.g., fogging, clearing stagnant water, wearing protective clothing)

7. The user can also click Take Required Actions to view an actionable checklist or call authorities directly.

**\[Alternate flow \- User location access denied\]**

2.A.1.1 App prompts the user to enable location services or manually select a location.

**\[Alternate flow \- No risk detected\]**

3.A.1 No notification is triggered; the app silently continues monitoring.

**\[Exceptions\]**

Not applicable.

**\[Business rules\]**

Not Applicable.

**\[Screenshots\]**

![][image31]![][image32]![][image33]

**\[Activity Diagram\]**

**![][image34]**

## **UC-13,1: PREDICT POTENTIAL DENGUE OUTBREAK** {#uc-13,1:-predict-potential-dengue-outbreak}

**\[List of actors\]**

1. Public User

2. Company User

**\[Pre-condition\]**

1. Historical and real-time data are available in the system

2. A trained prediction model is active and deployed

3. Data pipelines (e.g., drone images, dengue case records, weather feeds) are synchronized

**\[Post-condition\]**

1. Risk levels for defined geolocations are generated and stored

2. The system updates the dashboard, map views, and pushes alerts to relevant users

**\[Basic flow\]**

1. System detects new or updated data (dengue cases, weather metrics, drone imagery).

2. Data preprocessing and cleansing are performed.

3. Preprocessed data is fed into a trained machine learning model (e.g., Random Forest, LSTM).

4. Model predicts dengue outbreak risk scores for predefined zones.

5. Based on threshold values, zones are classified into:

   1. Low Risk

   2. Medium Risk

   3. High Risk

6. System stores predictions with metadata:

   1. Location

   2. Risk level

   3. Confidence Score

   4. Prediction Rate

7. Predictions are made available to:

   1. Admin Dashboard (UC10: Manage prediction & Alert)

   2. End-User notification engine(UC12: Get Potential Dengue Notification)

**\[Alternate flow \- Data Insufficient\]**

1.A.1.1 System logs the issue and skips prediction for that cycle.

**\[Alternate flow \- Model confidence below reliability threshold\]**

4.A.1.1 System marks the zone as “Uncertain” and excludes it from notifications.

**\[Exception flow \- Failed Model Execution\]**

4.E.1.1 System logs the error and falls back to the previous prediction data.

**\[Exception flow \- Misconfigured threshold values\]**

5.E.1.1 System halts classification and alerts the admin to review the configuration.

**\[Business rules\]**

Not Applicable

## **UC-14: GET RECOMMENDATIONS** {#uc-14:-get-recommendations}

**\[List of actors\]**

1. Public User

2. Company User

**\[Pre-condition\]**

1. The user is logged into the application.

**\[Post-condition\]**

1. The system displays the sample records according to descending or ascending order of sort values selected by User.

**\[Basic flow\]**

1. The user taps on the “Recommendation” icon from the navigation menu.

2. The system displays three buttons: High Risk, Medium Risk, and Low Risk Recommendation.

3. The user selects one of the risk levels.

4. The system displays a list of general recommendations related to the selected risk level.

5. The user may tap on each recommendation to view additional details

6. The user can tap the Back button (top-left) to return to the category selection screen and choose a different risk level.

**\[Alternate flow \- The user wants to explore other sections\]**

6.A.1.1 The user taps on another navigation icon (e.g., Dashboard or Profile).

6.A.1.2 The system redirects to the selected page.

**\[Exceptions\]**

Not applicable.

**\[Business rules\]**

Not Applicable

**\[Screenshots\]**

**![][image35]![][image36]![][image37]**

**\[Activity Diagram\]**

**![][image38]**

## 

## **UC-15: MONITOR DAILY DENGUE CASES** {#uc-15:-monitor-daily-dengue-cases}

**\[List of actors\]**

1. Public User

**\[Pre-condition\]**

1. User is logged in with companyId=comp-999, 

2. Location permission is granted

**\[Post-condition\]**

1. User can view and interact with daily dengue cases on the map

**\[Basic flow\]**

1. User opens the mobile app and navigates to the Dashboard page.

2. System checks if user has companyId='comp-999' (Public User).

3. System displays two tabs: "Current" and "Dengue Cases".

4. User taps on the "Dengue Cases" tab.

5. System fetches the latest dengue cases from the last 24 hours.

6. System displays a loading indicator while fetching data.

7. System displays all dengue cases as red markers on an interactive map.

8. Map automatically fits to show all available cases with appropriate padding.

9. User can interact with the map:

   1. Pan to explore different areas

   2. Zoom in/out to see more or less detail

   3. View user's current location (blue marker)

10. User can search for locations using the search bar positioned above the map.

11. System performs geocoding search and displays up to 5 matching results.

12. User selects a search result from the dropdown.

13. Map animates to center on the selected location.

14. User taps on a dengue case marker (red pin).

15. System displays a modal dialog with case details:

    1. Location name

    2. Date of report (formatted as "DD Month YYYY")

    3. Active cases count (highlighted in red)

    4. Total cases count (if available)

    5. Coverage area information (if available)

16. User can tap "View on Map" button to center the map on that specific case location.

17. User can close the modal by tapping the close button (X) or tapping outside the modal.

18. If user navigates away from their current location (\>200m), a "Return to Location" button appears.

19. User taps the "Return to Location" button.

20. Map animates back to center on user's current location.

**\[Exception Flow \- Server Error\]**

5.E.1.1 System Failed to fetch dengue cases

5.E.1.2 System displays error alert "Failed to load dengue cases"

**\[Alternate Flow \- User searches for location\]**

10.A.1.1 System displays search results dropdown,

10.A.1.2 User selects result

10.A.1.3 Map navigates to location

**\[Business rules\]**

Not Applicable.

**\[Screenshots\]**

**![][image39]**

## 

## **UC-16: CREATE DENGUE LOCATION ALERT** {#uc-16:-create-dengue-location-alert}

**\[List of actors\]**

1. Public User

**\[Pre-condition\]**

1. User is logged in and viewing the Dengue Cases tab

**\[Post-condition\]**

1. Location alert is created and saved, user can receive notifications for that location

**\[Basic flow\]**

1. User is viewing the Dengue Cases map with dengue case markers displayed.

2. User taps the "Location Alert" button (bottom-left floating button with notification icon).

3. System fetches user's existing location alerts.

4. System displays an alert options modal with two options:

   1. "View Existing Alerts" (shows count of saved alerts)

   2. "Create New Alert"

5. User taps "Create New Alert".

6. System displays the alert creation modal (slides up from bottom, 85% screen height).

7. User enters an alert name in the "Alert Name" field (e.g., "Home", "Office", "School").

8. User selects a location using one of two methods:

   1. Method A (Search): User types in the location search bar, system performs geocoding and displays results, user selects a result

   2. Method B (Map Tap): User taps directly on the interactive map to pin a location

9. System performs reverse geocoding to get the address name for the selected coordinates.

10. System displays the selected location with:

    1. Blue marker pin on the map

    2. Location address/name in a highlighted box below the map

11. System displays an informational note: "You will receive notifications when dengue cases are detected within approximately 500m of your pinned location."

12. User reviews the alert name and location.

13. User taps the "Create Alert" button.

14. System validates that both alert name and location are provided.

15. System creates the location alert with:

    1. Alert name

    2. Latitude and longitude coordinates

    3. Bounding box (500m radius)

    4. Address (from reverse geocoding)

    5. Active status (default: true)

16. System displays success message: "Location alert created successfully".

17. System closes the creation modal and refreshes the alerts list.

18. Alert is saved and user will receive notifications when dengue cases are detected near this location.

**\[Alternate Flow \- User taps "View Existing Alerts" \]**

4.A.1.1 The system displays modal with list of all saved alerts

4.A.1.2 User can toggle active/paused status, delete alerts, or create new alert

**\[Exception Flow \- Server Error \]**

13.E.1.1 The system Failed to create alert

13.E.1.2 The system displays error alert "Failed to create location alert"

**\[Business rules\]**

Not applicable.

**\[Screenshots\]**

**![][image40]![][image41]![][image42]**
